watershed.definition: A watershed (or catchment) is an area of land that drains all precipitation to a common outlet, such as the mouth of a river or a reservoir.

streamflow.definition: The volume of water flowing through a river channel at a given location, typically measured in cubic meters per second (m³/s) or cubic feet per second (cfs).

runoff_ratio.definition: The fraction of precipitation that appears as streamflow at the watershed outlet. Calculated as Q/P where Q is streamflow and P is precipitation.
runoff_ratio.interpretation: Low values indicate high evapotranspiration or infiltration; high values indicate efficient runoff generation

baseflow_index.definition: The proportion of streamflow derived from groundwater and delayed sources rather than direct surface runoff.
baseflow_index.interpretation: High BFI indicates significant groundwater contribution

aridity_index.definition: Ratio of potential evapotranspiration to precipitation. Indicates water availability relative to atmospheric demand.

drainage_density.definition: Total length of streams per unit area of watershed.

time_of_concentration.definition: The time required for water to travel from the most hydraulically remote point in the watershed to the outlet.

CAMELS.definition: Catchment Attributes and Meteorology for Large-sample Studies. A standardized dataset providing attributes for 671 watersheds across CONUS.
CAMELS.reference: Newman et al. (2015), Addor et al. (2017)

hydrological_signature.definition: Quantitative indices that characterize watershed behavior and hydrological response.

evapotranspiration.definition: Combined water loss from evaporation (soil/water surfaces) and transpiration (vegetation).
